metadata.name: agentic-workflow
metadata.summary: Agentic Flow is a platform for creating, managing, and executing AI workflows.

functions.name: alembic.env.include_object
documentation: other values from the config, defined by the needs of env.py,
can be acquired:
my_important_option = config.get_main_option("my_important_option")
... etc.

functions.name: alembic.env.run_migrations_offline
documentation:
Run migrations in 'offline' mode.

This configures the context with just a URL
and not an Engine, though an Engine is acceptable
here as well.  By skipping the Engine creation
we don't even need a DBAPI to be available.

Calls to context.execute() here emit the given string to the
script output.

functions.name: alembic.env.run_migrations_online
documentation:
Run migrations in 'online' mode.

In this scenario we need to create an Engine
and associate a connection with the context.

functions.name: src.main.create_app
documentation:
Factory function to create the FastAPI application.
Can be used either as a standalone app or as a library component.

Args:
    auth_provider: Custom authentication provider. If None, uses NoAuthProvider
    title: API title
    description: API description
    version: API version
    **kwargs: Additional FastAPI configuration parameters

functions.name: src.utils.helpers.generateRandomId
documentation:
Generates a random alphanumeric ID with the given prefix.
Args:
    prefix (str): The prefix to add to the generated ID.
    length (int): The length of the alphanumeric part of the ID. Default is 8.
Returns:
    str: A string containing the prefix followed by a random alphanumeric ID.

classes.name: src.utils.helpers.IdPrefix
documentation: IdPrefix length should be three char

functions.name: src.utils.auth.get_current_user
documentation:
Dependency that handles authentication and authorization.

Usage:
    @router.post("/")
    async def create_item(user: User = Depends(get_current_user)):
        # user object is available here

classes.name: src.utils.auth.User
documentation: Protocol defining the required user attributes

classes.name: src.utils.auth.AuthProvider

methods.name: src.utils.auth.AuthProvider.get_user_from_token
documentation:
Authenticate and return a user from a token.

Args:
    credentials: The authorization credentials
    request: The incoming request object
    
Returns:
    Optional[User]: The authenticated user or None if authentication fails
    
Raises:
    HTTPException: If authentication fails

methods.name: src.utils.auth.AuthProvider.authorize
documentation:
Check if a user has permission to perform the requested action.

Args:
    user: The authenticated user
    request: The incoming request
    
Returns:
    bool: True if authorized, False otherwise
Abstract base class for authentication providers.
